1	# Distilled domain summary ŌĆö `fathomdb-py` binding + STATUS-0.8.9
2	
3	Faithful, dense technical distillation of three source files:
4	
5	1. `src/rust/crates/fathomdb-py/Cargo.toml`
6	2. `src/rust/crates/fathomdb-py/src/lib.rs` (1489 lines)
7	3. `dev/plans/runs/STATUS-0.8.9.md`
8	
9	All names/values preserved so questions can be answered without the originals. Terse prose, exhaustive on identifiers.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## 1. `fathomdb-py/Cargo.toml`
14	
15	### Package
16	- `name = "fathomdb-py"`.
17	- `version`, `edition`, `license`, `repository`, `rust-version` all `.workspace = true`.
18	- `publish = false` (this crate is not published).
19	
20	### `[lib]`
21	- `name = "fathomdb_py"`.
22	- `crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]`.
23	
24	### `[features]`
25	Five feature entries (plus `default`):
26	
27	| Feature | Value | Forwards to engine? | cfg-gated in lib.rs? |
28	|---|---|---|---|
29	| `default` | `[]` (empty) | ŌĆö | n/a |
30	| `test-hooks` | `[]` (empty, no deps) | no | **YES** ŌĆö `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-hooks"))]` gates the test-only functions (see ┬¦2 test hooks) |
31	| `default-embedder` | `["fathomdb-engine/default-embedder"]` | yes | **NO** direct `#[cfg(feature="default-embedder")]` in lib.rs ŌĆö it is a pure engine-forward; the `use_default_embedder=True` kwarg materializes a real `CandleBgeEmbedder` only when this feature is on, else rejects `True` at open time (per ADR-0.7.1-default-embedder-weight-fetch; no silent network in a no-feature wheel) |
32	| `default-reranker` | `["fathomdb-engine/default-reranker"]` | yes | **NO** direct cfg in lib.rs ŌĆö pure engine-forward. A plain `maturin develop` / `pytest` activates the real CE reranker in dev/test; the shipped wheel keeps it OFF (release.yml and ci.yml pin explicit `--features` lists omitting it). Added in 0.8.2 Slice E2 fix-1 [P1]. |
33	| `embed-cuda` | `["default-embedder", "fathomdb-engine/embed-cuda"]` | yes (and pulls `default-embedder`) | **NO** direct cfg in lib.rs ŌĆö pure engine-forward. GPU opt-in; default wheel stays CPU. Build: `maturin develop --features pyo3/extension-module,embed-cuda`; activate via `FATHOMDB_EMBED_DEVICE=cuda`. See `dev/design/0.8.1-embedder-gpu-and-portability.md` |
34	| `embed-metal` | `["default-embedder", "fathomdb-engine/embed-metal"]` | yes (and pulls `default-embedder`) | **NO** direct cfg in lib.rs ŌĆö pure engine-forward |
35	
36	**Key cfg distinction:** ONLY `test-hooks` (plus the built-in `test`) is referenced via `#[cfg(...)]` inside lib.rs. The other four features (`default-embedder`, `default-reranker`, `embed-cuda`, `embed-metal`) are NOT cfg-gated in the binding source ŌĆö they exist purely to forward Cargo features down to `fathomdb-engine`; their runtime effect surfaces only through engine behavior (e.g. `EmbedderChoice::Default` succeeding vs. erroring).
37	
38	### `[dependencies]`
39	- `fathomdb-embedder.workspace = true`
40	- `fathomdb-embedder-api.workspace = true`
41	- `fathomdb-engine.workspace = true`
42	- `fathomdb-schema.workspace = true`
43	- `pyo3 = { version = "0.29", features = ["extension-module", "abi3-py310"] }`  ŌåÉ **pin = "0.29"; features = `extension-module` + `abi3-py310`**
44	- `serde_json = "1"`
45	
46	(Note: lib.rs top comment still references "pyo3 0.22" macro behavior and the module comment references "pyo3 0.28" free-threaded default ŌĆö stale prose; the actual pin is **0.29**. This pin came from the 0.8.8 pyo3 0.24.1ŌåÆ0.29.0 migration / Dependabot PR #89, RUSTSEC-2026-0176/0177.)
47	
48	---
49	
50	## 2. `fathomdb-py/src/lib.rs` (1489 lines)
51	
52	PyO3 binding from the Python SDK to `fathomdb-engine`. Module is named `_fathomdb`; classes declare `module = "fathomdb._fathomdb"`.
53	
54	### Crate-level attributes (lines 1-8)
55	- `#![allow(unexpected_cfgs)]` ŌĆö pyo3 macros emit `#[cfg(feature = "gil-refs")]` arms referencing an upstream feature this crate does not export; suppresses noise on a clippy `-D warnings` gate.
56	- `#![allow(clippy::useless_conversion)]` ŌĆö covers `#[pymethods]`-generated `PyResult` wrappers flagged as redundant `Into<PyErr>`.
57	
58	### FFI safety contract (module doc)
59	1. Every method that may block in the engine wraps the call in `py.detach(...)` to release the GIL.
60	2. Engine entry points return typed errors via `engine_error_to_py` / `engine_open_error_to_py` ŌĆö single-switch mapping, **no catch-all arm**; binding fails to compile if the Rust variant set drifts from the Python class set (AC-060a).
61	3. Every string crossing FFI is checked by `validate_ffi_string` for embedded NUL or unpaired UTF-16 surrogate BEFORE the writer transaction opens (AC-068a / AC-068b).
62	4. Panics inside engine code surface as Python `PanicException` (`pyo3::panic::PanicException`); host process not aborted (AC-067). Engine calls wrapped in `catch_unwind` so the panic is translated Rust-side. **`PanicException` is intentionally NOT an `EngineError` subclass** ŌĆö panic is a contract bug, not a typed engine outcome.
63	
64	### Imports
65	- `std::panic::{catch_unwind, AssertUnwindSafe}`, `std::sync::Arc`.
66	- `fathomdb_embedder::EmbedderEvent as RustEmbedderEvent`.
67	- `fathomdb_embedder_api::EmbedderIdentity as RustEmbedderIdentity`.
68	- From `fathomdb_engine`: `rerank_passages as rust_rerank_passages`, `ComparisonOp as RustComparisonOp`, `CorruptionDetail`, `CorruptionKind`, `EmbedderChoice`, `Engine as RustEngine`, `EngineError as RustEngineError`, `EngineOpenError`, `ExtractDocument as RustExtractDocument`, `IngestWithExtractorReceipt as RustIngestWithExtractorReceipt`, `NodeRecord as RustNodeRecord`, `OpStoreRow as RustOpStoreRow`, `OpenReport as RustOpenReport`, `OpenStage`, `Predicate as RustPredicate`, `PreparedWrite`, `ScalarValue as RustScalarValue`, `SearchExpandResult as RustSearchExpandResult`, `SearchFilter as RustSearchFilter`, `SearchHit as RustSearchHit`, `SearchResult as RustSearchResult`, `SoftFallback as RustSoftFallback`, `SoftFallbackBranch`, `TraversalDirection as RustTraversalDirection`, `WriteReceipt as RustWriteReceipt`.
69	- `fathomdb_schema::MigrationStepReport as RustMigrationStepReport`.
70	- pyo3: `create_exception`, `exceptions::{PyException, PyTypeError, PyValueError}`, `panic::PanicException`, `prelude::*`, `types::{PyDict, PyList}`.
71	
72	### Exception type hierarchy (`create_exception!`, lines 62-86)
73	Root: **`EngineError`** inherits Python **`PyException`** (i.e. `Exception`). All concrete leaves inherit from `EngineError`, EXCEPT where noted nested. Declared in module `_fathomdb`. Full list with parent:
74	
75	- `EngineError` ŌåÉ `PyException`  (root)
76	- `StorageError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
77	- `ProjectionError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
78	- `VectorError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
79	- `KindNotVectorIndexedError` ŌåÉ **`VectorError`**  (nested under VectorError, not EngineError directly)
80	- `EmbedderError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
81	- `EmbedderNotConfiguredError` ŌåÉ **`EmbedderError`**  (nested under EmbedderError)
82	- `SchedulerError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
83	- `OpStoreError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
84	- `WriteValidationError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
85	- `SchemaValidationError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
86	- `OverloadedError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
87	- `ClosingError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
88	- `DatabaseLockedError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
89	- `CorruptionError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
90	- `IncompatibleSchemaVersionError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
91	- `MigrationError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
92	- `EmbedderIdentityMismatchError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
93	- `EmbedderDimensionMismatchError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`
94	- `ExtractorError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`  (G11 / Slice 15 ŌĆö BYO-LLM extraction harness protocol error)
95	- `InvalidFilterError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`  (G4 / Slice 35 ŌĆö filter predicate construction, non-allowlisted path)
96	- `InvalidArgumentError` ŌåÉ `EngineError`  (Slice 20 G5/G6 ŌĆö traversal depth > 3 / out-of-range arg)
97	
98	So the two two-level leaves are `KindNotVectorIndexedError` (under `VectorError`) and `EmbedderNotConfiguredError` (under `EmbedderError`). 22 `create_exception!` macros total; `PanicException` is separate (NOT in this tree).
99	
100	### String validation (AC-068a/AC-068b)
101	- `pub fn validate_ffi_string(value: &str) -> Result<(), String>`: rejects embedded NUL byte (`"embedded NUL byte in FFI string"`) and unpaired UTF-16 surrogate `U+D800..=U+DFFF` (`"unpaired UTF-16 surrogate U+{cp:04X} in FFI string"`).
102	- `fn validate_ffi_string_py(value: &str) -> PyResult<()>`: maps the err to `WriteValidationError::new_err`.
103	- `fn extract_validated_str(value: &Bound<PyAny>) -> PyResult<String>`: extracts a Python str into Rust `String` + validates. On extraction failure (lone surrogate ŌåÆ PyO3 `UnicodeEncodeError`) re-raises as `WriteValidationError` ("string contains characters not representable as UTF-8 (lone surrogate)").
104	- `fn extract_opt_validated_str(value: Option<&Bound<PyAny>>) -> PyResult<Option<String>>`: `None`/None-valued stays `None` (preserves all-`None` byte-identical unfiltered path); present value extracted + validated. Used by `search` for G10 `SearchFilter` string fields.
105	
106	### Error mapping
107	- `fn engine_error_to_py(err: RustEngineError) -> PyErr` (no catch-all). Variant ŌåÆ Python class:
108	  - `Storage` ŌåÆ `StorageError` ("storage error")
109	  - `Projection` ŌåÆ `ProjectionError` ("projection error")
110	  - `Vector` ŌåÆ `VectorError` ("vector error")
111	  - `Embedder` ŌåÆ `EmbedderError` ("embedder error")
112	  - `EmbedderNotConfigured` ŌåÆ `EmbedderNotConfiguredError` ("embedder is not configured")
113	  - `KindNotVectorIndexed` ŌåÆ `KindNotVectorIndexedError`
114	  - `EmbedderDimensionMismatch { expected, actual }` ŌåÆ `EmbedderDimensionMismatchError`; sets attrs `stored = expected`, `supplied = actual`
115	  - `Scheduler` ŌåÆ `SchedulerError`
116	  - `OpStore` ŌåÆ `OpStoreError`
117	  - `WriteValidation` ŌåÆ `WriteValidationError`
118	  - `SchemaValidation` ŌåÆ `SchemaValidationError`
119	  - `Overloaded` ŌåÆ `OverloadedError` ("engine overloaded")
120	  - `Closing` ŌåÆ `ClosingError` ("engine is closing")
121	  - `Extractor` ŌåÆ `ExtractorError`
122	  - `InvalidFilter { reason }` ŌåÆ `InvalidFilterError` ("invalid filter: {reason}")
123	  - `InvalidArgument { msg }` ŌåÆ `InvalidArgumentError` (msg verbatim)
124	- `fn corruption_kind_str(CorruptionKind) -> &'static str`: `WalReplayFailure`, `HeaderMalformed`, `SchemaInconsistent`, `EmbedderIdentityDrift`.
125	- `fn open_stage_str(OpenStage) -> &'static str`: `HeaderProbe`, `WalReplay`, `SchemaProbe`, `EmbedderIdentity`.
126	- `fn engine_open_error_to_py(err: EngineOpenError) -> PyErr`. Variants:
127	  - `DatabaseLocked { holder_pid }` ŌåÆ `DatabaseLockedError`; sets attr `holder_pid`. Message "database is locked by process {pid}" or "...by another engine instance".
128	  - `Corruption(detail)` ŌåÆ `corruption_to_py(detail)`
129	  - `IncompatibleSchemaVersion { seen, supported }` ŌåÆ `IncompatibleSchemaVersionError`
130	  - `MigrationError { schema_version_before, schema_version_current, step_id }` ŌåÆ `MigrationError`
131	  - `EmbedderIdentityMismatch { stored, supplied }` ŌåÆ `EmbedderIdentityMismatchError`; sets attrs `stored_name`, `stored_revision`, `supplied_name`, `supplied_revision`
132	  - `EmbedderDimensionMismatch { stored, supplied }` ŌåÆ `EmbedderDimensionMismatchError`; sets attrs `stored`, `supplied`
133	  - `Embedder(err)` ŌåÆ `EmbedderError` ("{err:?}")
134	  - `Io { message }` ŌåÆ `StorageError` ("database I/O error: {message}")
135	- `fn corruption_to_py(detail: CorruptionDetail) -> PyErr`: builds `CorruptionError` "corruption {kind} at stage {stage} ({recovery_hint_code})"; sets attrs `kind`, `stage`, `recovery_hint_code`, `doc_anchor` (from `detail.recovery_hint.code` / `.doc_anchor`).
136	- `fn call_engine<R: Send>(py, f: impl FnOnce() -> Result<R, RustEngineError> + Send) -> PyResult<R>`: wraps `f` in `AssertUnwindSafe`, runs `py.detach(|| catch_unwind(wrapped))`. `Ok(Ok)` ŌåÆ value; `Ok(Err)` ŌåÆ `engine_error_to_py`; `Err(_)` (panic) ŌåÆ `PanicException::new_err("engine panic (see logs)")`. Uses `AssertUnwindSafe` because the engine's `Arc<dyn Embedder>` makes the natural `UnwindSafe` bound unsatisfiable.
137	
138	### Data classes (`#[pyclass]` types)
139	All declare `module = "fathomdb._fathomdb"` (except the Slice-20 pair which omit module), most are `frozen, get_all, skip_from_py_object` and `#[derive(Clone)]`. Full list of pyclass types and fields:
140	
141	1. **`PyWriteReceipt`** (name `"WriteReceipt"`): `cursor: u64`, `row_cursors: Vec<u64>` (G0/Slice 15 ŌĆö per-row write_cursors 1:1 with input batch order), `dangling_edge_endpoints: u64` (G8/Slice 20 ŌĆö count of edge endpoints pointing at non-existent/superseded canonical node; flag-and-count). `from_rust(RustWriteReceipt)`.
142	2. **`PyIngestWithExtractorReceipt`** (name `"IngestWithExtractorReceipt"`, G11/Slice 15): `nodes_written: u64`, `edges_written: u64`, `docs_processed: u64`. `from_rust(RustIngestWithExtractorReceipt)`.
143	3. **`PySoftFallback`** (name `"SoftFallback"`): `branch: String`. `from_rust(&RustSoftFallback)` maps `SoftFallbackBranch::{VectorŌåÆ"vector", TextŌåÆ"text", TextEdgeŌåÆ"text_edge", GraphArmŌåÆ"graph_arm"}`.
144	4. **`PySearchHit`** (name `"SearchHit"`): `id: u64`, `kind: String`, `body: String`, `score: f64`, `branch: String`, `source_id: Option<String>` (G0 Phase-2 ŌĆö set only for graph-arm hits, None for two-arm hits), `ce_score: Option<f64>` (0.8.5 EXP-0 ŌĆö per-candidate `ce_norm = sigmoid(ce_logit)`; Some only inside reranked pool). `from_rust(&RustSearchHit)`.
145	5. **`PySearchResult`** (name `"SearchResult"`): `projection_cursor: u64`, `soft_fallback: Option<PySoftFallback>`, `results: Vec<PySearchHit>`. `from_rust(RustSearchResult)`.
146	6. **`PyNodeRecord`** (name `"NodeRecord"`): `logical_id: String`, `kind: String`, `body: String`, `write_cursor: u64`. `from_rust(&RustNodeRecord)`.
147	7. **`PyOpStoreRow`** (name `"OpStoreRow"`): `id: i64`, `collection: String`, `record_key: String`, `op_kind: String`, `payload: String`, `schema_id: Option<String>`, `write_cursor: u64`. `from_rust(&RustOpStoreRow)`.
148	8. **`PyCounterSnapshot`** (name `"CounterSnapshot"`): `queries: u64`, `writes: u64`, `write_rows: u64`, `admin_ops: u64`, `cache_hit: u64`, `cache_miss: u64`. (No `from_rust`; built inline in `PyEngine::counters`.)
149	9. **`PyMigrationStepReport`** (name `"MigrationStepReport"`): `step_id: u32`, `duration_ms: Option<u64>`, `failed: bool`. `from_rust(&RustMigrationStepReport)`.
150	10. **`PyEmbedderIdentity`** (name `"EmbedderIdentity"`): `name: String`, `revision: String`, `dimension: u32`. `from_rust(&RustEmbedderIdentity)`.
151	11. **`PyOpenReport`** (name `"OpenReport"`, `frozen, get_all` ŌĆö NOT `skip_from_py_object`, NOT `Clone`): `schema_version_before: u32`, `schema_version_after: u32`, `migration_steps: Vec<PyMigrationStepReport>`, `embedder_warmup_ms: u64`, `query_backend: String`, `default_embedder: PyEmbedderIdentity`, `embedder_download_ms: Option<u64>` (EU-3 loader wall-time fetching default-embedder weights; None on full cache hit/caller-supplied), `embedder_events: Vec<Py<PyAny>>` (structured loader events as dicts keyed by "kind"), `embedder_mean_centering_required: bool` (static ŌĆö true when default embedder needs mean-centering e.g. bge-small), `embedder_mean_vec_pinned: bool` (dynamic ŌĆö true iff `_fathomdb_embedder_profiles.mean_vec IS NOT NULL`). `from_rust(py, &RustOpenReport)`.
152	12. **`PyExpandedNode`** (name `"ExpandedNode"`, Slice 20; `skip_from_py_object`, `Clone`; fields use per-field `#[pyo3(get)]` not `get_all`; NO `module=`): `node: PyNodeRecord`, `hop_count: u32`.
153	13. **`PySearchExpandResult`** (name `"SearchExpandResult"`, Slice 20 G6; `skip_from_py_object`, `Clone`; per-field `#[pyo3(get)]`; NO `module=`): `search_hits: Vec<PySearchHit>`, `expanded: Vec<PyExpandedNode>`, `all_logical_ids: Vec<String>`. `from_rust(RustSearchExpandResult)` ŌĆö `expanded` built from `(node, hop_count)` tuples.
154	
155	### `embedder_event_to_py(py, ev: &RustEmbedderEvent) -> Py<PyAny>`
156	Serializes one event as a Python dict (so callers pattern-match on `"kind"` without importing leaf classes). Variants and their dict keys:
157	- `DefaultEmbedderDownload { file, url, bytes, sha256, cache_path, duration_ms }` ŌåÆ kind `"DefaultEmbedderDownload"` + keys `file`, `url`, `bytes`, `sha256`, `cache_path` (display string), `duration_ms`.
158	- `DefaultEmbedderCacheHit { file, sha256, cache_path }` ŌåÆ kind `"DefaultEmbedderCacheHit"` + `file`, `sha256`, `cache_path`.
159	- `MeanVecPinned { dim, doc_count }` ŌåÆ kind `"MeanVecPinned"` + `dim`, `doc_count`.
160	- `MeanVecRecomputed { dim, doc_count, trigger }` ŌåÆ kind `"MeanVecRecomputed"` + `dim`, `doc_count`, `trigger` (`trigger.as_str()`).
161	
162	### `PyEngine` (`#[pyclass] name = "Engine"`)
163	Fields: `inner: Arc<RustEngine>`, `open_report: Arc<RustOpenReport>`.
164	
165	`#[pymethods]` ŌĆö full method list:
166	- **`open`** (`#[staticmethod]`, signature `(path, use_default_embedder = false)`): validates path; `py.detach` + `catch_unwind` calls `RustEngine::open_with_choice(path, choice)` where `choice = if use_default_embedder { EmbedderChoice::Default } else { EmbedderChoice::None }` (EU-6: True ŌåÆ engine materializes pinned bge-small via EU-3 loader; False ŌåÆ engine opens but vector writes fail `EmbedderNotConfigured`). Panic during open ŌåÆ `PanicException::new_err("engine panic during open")`; open error ŌåÆ `engine_open_error_to_py`.
167	- **`open_report(&self, py) -> PyOpenReport`**: `PyOpenReport::from_rust(py, &self.open_report)`.
168	- **`write(&self, py, batch: Bound<PyList>) -> PyResult<PyWriteReceipt>`**: `translate_batch` ŌåÆ `call_engine(... engine.write(&prepared))`.
169	- **`search`** (signature `(query, source_type=None, kind=None, created_after=None, status=None, rerank_depth=0, use_graph_arm=false, alpha=None, pool_n=None)`; `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]`): G10 + 0.8.1 R1 hybrid search. Validates query + filter strings via `extract_opt_validated_str`. Builds `Some(RustSearchFilter { source_type, kind, created_after, status })` only if any of source_type/kind/created_after/status is set, else `None` (all-None = byte-identical unfiltered). Defaults resolved binding-side: `alpha = alpha.unwrap_or(0.3)`, `pool_n = pool_n.unwrap_or(rerank_depth)`. Calls `engine.search_reranked(&query, filter, rerank_depth, use_graph_arm, alpha, pool_n)`. `rerank_depth=0` is no-op identity; `>0` activates CE path (needs `default-reranker` feature + model loaded, else falls back to identity). `use_graph_arm=true` (0.8.1 R3 / Slice 30) seeds BFS over temporal fact-edges from top-10 fused hits as a 3rd RRF arm. 0.8.5 EXP-0: `alpha` default 0.3 clamped [0,1] in engine; `alpha=1.0, pool_n=10` is the measured-parity config.
170	- **`close(&self, py) -> PyResult<()>`**: `call_engine(... engine.close())`.
171	- **`drain`** (signature `(timeout_s = 0.0)`): converts finite `timeout_s>0` to ms (`(timeout_s*1000.0) as u64`) else 0; `call_engine(... engine.drain(ms))`.
172	- **`ingest_with_extractor(&self, py, cmd: Bound<PyList>, documents: Bound<PyList>) -> PyResult<PyIngestWithExtractorReceipt>`** (G11/Slice 15 BYO-LLM): `cmd` = argv (must be strings, else `WriteValidationError` "cmd elements must be strings"); `documents` = list of dicts with required `source_doc_id` + `body` (else "document must be a dict" / missing-field error) ŌåÆ `Vec<RustExtractDocument>`. Calls `engine.ingest_with_extractor(&cmd_refs, &docs)`.
173	- **`counters(&self) -> PyCounterSnapshot`**: reads `self.inner.counters()`, builds `PyCounterSnapshot` inline.
174	- **`set_profiling(&self, enabled: bool) -> PyResult<()>`**: `self.inner.set_profiling(enabled)` mapped via `engine_error_to_py`.
175	- **`set_slow_threshold_ms(&self, value: u64) -> PyResult<()>`**: `self.inner.set_slow_threshold_ms(value)`.
176	- **`embed(&self, py, text: &str) -> PyResult<Vec<f32>>`**: validates text; `call_engine(... engine.embed_text(&text))`. Uses pinned default embedder `fathomdb-bge-small-en-v1.5`; raises `EmbedderNotConfiguredError` if opened without embedder.
177	- **`_configure_vector_kind_for_test(&self, py, kind: &str) -> PyResult<()>`** ŌĆö **TEST-HOOKS-GATED** `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-hooks"))]`. EU-6 vector-write seam. `engine.configure_vector_kind_for_test(&kind)`.
178	- **`_write_vector_for_test(&self, py, kind: &str, text: &str) -> PyResult<()>`** ŌĆö **TEST-HOOKS-GATED** `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-hooks"))]`. `engine.write_vector_for_test(&kind, &text)`.
179	- **`attach_logging_subscriber`** (signature `(logger, heartbeat_interval_ms = None)`): currently a no-op (`let _ = logger; let _ = heartbeat_interval_ms; Ok(())`); subscriber wiring deferred to a later 0.6.x slice.
180	
181	### Standalone `#[pyfunction]`s (module-level, NOT engine methods)
182	1. **`admin_configure`** (signature `(engine, name, body)`): admin.configure verb. Validates name+body; name non-empty (else `PyValueError` "admin.configure requires a non-empty name"). Builds `PreparedWrite::AdminSchema { name, kind: "latest_state", schema_json: body, retention_json: "{}" }`; `call_engine(... inner.write(&batch))` ŌåÆ `PyWriteReceipt`.
183	2. **`read_get`** (signature `(engine, logical_id)`, G2/G3 Slice 30): active-only point lookup by logical_id; not-found = `None` (NOT an exception ŌĆö typed NotFound reserved for Slice 31). Returns `Option<PyNodeRecord>` via `inner.read_get(&logical_id)`.
184	3. **`read_get_many`** (signature `(engine, logical_ids)`): extracts+validates each id; `inner.read_get_many(&ids)` ŌåÆ `Vec<Option<PyNodeRecord>>`.
185	4. **`read_collection`** (signature `(engine, collection, after_id=None, limit=0)`): paginated op-store read-back via `read_collection_impl` ŌåÆ `inner.read_collection(&collection, after_id, limit)` ŌåÆ `Vec<PyOpStoreRow>`.
186	5. **`read_mutations`** (signature `(engine, collection, after_id=None, limit=0)`): same impl as `read_collection` (both call `read_collection_impl`). Mandatory limit + after_id cursor; rides engine ReaderWorkerPool DEFERRED-tx path.
187	6. **`read_list`** (signature `(engine, kind, predicates=None, limit=100)`, G4/Slice 35): lists active canonical nodes of `kind`, optional `Predicate` dict filters. Each predicate dict shape `{ "type": "eq"|"gt"|"gte"|"lt"|"lte", "path": str, "value": str|int|bool }`. `inner.read_list(&kind, &rust_predicates, limit)` ŌåÆ `Vec<PyNodeRecord>`.
188	7. **`graph_neighbors`** (signature `(engine, logical_id, depth, direction)`, Slice 20 G5): bounded BFS over `canonical_edges`. `depth` 1..=3 (>3 ŌåÆ `InvalidArgumentError`); direction `"outgoing"|"incoming"|"both"`. Returns reachable nodes (excl. root), hard-capped at 50. `inner.graph_neighbors(&logical_id, depth, dir)`.
189	8. **`search_expand`** (signature `(engine, query, depth, source_type=None, kind=None, created_after=None, status=None)`, Slice 20 G6; `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]`): hybrid search then bounded BFS expansion. `depth` 0..=3 (>3 ŌåÆ `InvalidArgumentError`). Same filter-build pattern as `search`. `inner.search_expand(&query, filter, depth)` ŌåÆ `PySearchExpandResult`.
190	9. **`rerank`** (signature `(query, passages, rerank_depth, alpha=None, pool_n=None)`, 0.8.2 Slice E2): standalone CE rerank over a caller-supplied passage list ŌĆö NOT engine-bound. Marshals `[{"id": int, "body": str, "score": float}]` into `(id, body, score)` tuples; calls pure `rust_rerank_passages(&query, tuples, rerank_depth, alpha, pool_n)` inside `py.detach` + `catch_unwind`. Defaults `alpha = alpha.unwrap_or(0.3)`, `pool_n = pool_n.unwrap_or(rerank_depth)`. Returns `Vec<Py<PyDict>>` of `{"id", "score", "ce_score"}` (ce_score None outside reranked pool). Panic ŌåÆ `PanicException` ("rerank panic (see logs)"); inner Err (non-finite score) ŌåÆ `WriteValidationError`. E2 fix-1 [P2]: helper returns `Result<Vec<ŌĆ”>, String>`.

# Distilled domain summary ŌĆö `fathomdb-py` binding + STATUS-0.8.9

Faithful, dense technical distillation of three source files:

1. `src/rust/crates/fathomdb-py/Cargo.toml`
2. `src/rust/crates/fathomdb-py/src/lib.rs` (1489 lines)
3. `dev/plans/runs/STATUS-0.8.9.md`

All names/values preserved so questions can be answered without the originals. Terse prose, exhaustive on identifiers.

---

## 1. `fathomdb-py/Cargo.toml`

### Package
- `name = "fathomdb-py"`.
- `version`, `edition`, `license`, `repository`, `rust-version` all `.workspace = true`.
- `publish = false` (this crate is not published).

### `[lib]`
- `name = "fathomdb_py"`.
- `crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]`.

### `[features]`
Five feature entries (plus `default`):

| Feature | Value | Forwards to engine? | cfg-gated in lib.rs? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `default` | `[]` (empty) | ŌĆö | n/a |
| `test-hooks` | `[]` (empty, no deps) | no | **YES** ŌĆö `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-hooks"))]` gates the test-only functions (see ┬¦2 test hooks) |
| `default-embedder` | `["fathomdb-engine/default-embedder"]` | yes | **NO** direct `#[cfg(feature="default-embedder")]` in lib.rs ŌĆö it is a pure engine-forward; the `use_default_embedder=True` kwarg materializes a real `CandleBgeEmbedder` only when this feature is on, else rejects `True` at open time (per ADR-0.7.1-default-embedder-weight-fetch; no silent network in a no-feature wheel) |
| `default-reranker` | `["fathomdb-engine/default-reranker"]` | yes | **NO** direct cfg in lib.rs ŌĆö pure engine-forward. A plain `maturin develop` / `pytest` activates the real CE reranker in dev/test; the shipped wheel keeps it OFF (release.yml and ci.yml pin explicit `--features` lists omitting it). Added in 0.8.2 Slice E2 fix-1 [P1]. |

**Key cfg distinction:** ONLY `test-hooks` (plus the built-in `test`) is referenced via `#[cfg(...)]` inside lib.rs. The other four features (`default-embedder`, `default-reranker`, `embed-cuda`, `embed-metal`) are NOT cfg-gated in the binding source ŌĆö they exist purely to forward Cargo features down to `fathomdb-engine`; their runtime effect surfaces only through engine behavior (e.g. `EmbedderChoice::Default` succeeding vs. erroring).

## 2. `fathomdb-py/src/lib.rs` (1489 lines)

PyO3 binding from the Python SDK to `fathomdb-engine`. Module is named `_fathomdb`; classes declare `module = "fathomdb._fathomdb"`.

### Crate-level attributes (lines 1-8)
- `#![allow(unexpected_cfgs)]` ŌĆö pyo3 macros emit `#[cfg(feature = "gil-refs")]` arms referencing an upstream feature this crate does not export; suppresses noise on a clippy `-D warnings` gate.
- `#![allow(clippy::useless_conversion)]` ŌĆö covers `#[pymethods]`-generated `PyResult` wrappers flagged as redundant `Into<PyErr>`.

### FFI safety contract (module doc)
1. Every method that may block in the engine wraps the call in `py.detach(...)` to release the GIL.
2. Engine entry points return typed errors via `engine_error_to_py` / `engine_open_error_to_py` ŌĆö single-switch mapping, **no catch-all arm**; binding fails to compile if the Rust variant set drifts from the Python class set (AC-060a).
3. Every string crossing FFI is checked by `validate_ffi_string` for embedded NUL or unpaired UTF-16 surrogate BEFORE the writer transaction opens (AC-068a / AC-068b).
4. Panics inside engine code surface as Python `PanicException` (`pyo3::panic::PanicException`); host process not aborted (AC-067). Engine calls wrapped in `catch_unwind` so the panic is translated Rust-side. **`PanicException` is intentionally NOT an `EngineError` subclass** ŌĆö panic is a contract bug, not a typed engine outcome.

`#[pymethods]` ŌĆö full method list:

- `translate_write_item(&Bound<PyAny>) -> PreparedWrite`: dispatches on dict keys `edge` ŌåÆ `translate_edge`, `op_store` ŌåÆ `translate_op_store`, `admin_schema` ŌåÆ `translate_admin_schema`, `node` ŌåÆ `translate_node`; bare `{"kind": ...}` shape treated as a Node.
- `translate_node` ŌåÆ `PreparedWrite::Node { kind (required), body (default "{}"), source_id (opt), logical_id (opt) }`.
- `translate_edge` ŌåÆ `PreparedWrite::Edge { kind, from, to (all required), source_id, logical_id, body (opt ŌĆö relation text projected into `search_index_edges`), t_valid, t_invalid (opt ISO-8601, R3/Slice 30 temporal validity), confidence: None, extractor_model_id: None, temporal_fallback: None }`.
- `translate_op_store` ŌåÆ `PreparedWrite::OpStore { collection, record_key (required), schema_id (opt), body (required) }`.
- `translate_admin_schema` ŌåÆ `PreparedWrite::AdminSchema { name, kind, schema_json (required), retention_json (default "{}") }`; non-dict ŌåÆ `PyTypeError` (note: not WriteValidationError here).
- `py_predicate_to_rust(&Bound<PyAny>) -> RustPredicate`: reads `type`/`path`/`value`. Value coercion order: **bool first** (Python bool is int subclass ŌåÆ must check before int), then `i64`, else `Text` (validated). `RustScalarValue::{Bool, Integer, Text}`. Maps type ŌåÆ `RustPredicate::json_path_eq` (eq) or `json_path_compare(path, RustComparisonOp::{Gt|Gte|Lt|Lte}, scalar)`; unknown type ŌåÆ `PyValueError` ("unknown predicate type '{other}'; expected 'eq', 'gt', 'gte', 'lt', or 'lte'").
- `parse_direction(&str) -> RustTraversalDirection`: `"outgoing"ŌåÆOutgoing`, `"incoming"ŌåÆIncoming`, `"both"ŌåÆBoth`, else `InvalidArgumentError`.
- `read_collection_impl(py, engine, collection, after_id, limit)`.

### `#[pymodule]` registration ŌĆö **`gil_used = true`**
Declared `#[pymodule(gil_used = true)]` on `fn _fathomdb(py, m: Bound<PyModule>) -> PyResult<()>`. **`gil_used = true`** preserves current GIL semantics (pyo3 0.28+ makes `#[pymodule]` free-threaded by default, but this binding is `abi3-py310` and the whole FFI contract assumes GIL held; free-threading `gil_used = false` is a separate larger campaign ŌĆö see `dev/design/free-threaded-python-value-lift-and-experiments.md`).

Registered classes (in order): `PyEngine`, `PyWriteReceipt`, `PyIngestWithExtractorReceipt`, `PySoftFallback`, `PySearchHit`, `PySearchResult`, `PyCounterSnapshot`, `PyMigrationStepReport`, `PyEmbedderIdentity`, `PyOpenReport`, `PyNodeRecord`, `PyOpStoreRow`, `PyExpandedNode`, `PySearchExpandResult`.

Registered functions: `admin_configure`, `read_get`, `read_get_many`, `read_collection`, `read_mutations`, `read_list`, `graph_neighbors`, `search_expand`, `rerank`, and (cfg-gated) `force_panic_for_test`.

Registered exception type objects via `m.add("<Name>", py.get_type::<Name>())` for all 22 exceptions (EngineError, StorageError, ProjectionError, VectorError, KindNotVectorIndexedError, EmbedderError, EmbedderNotConfiguredError, SchedulerError, OpStoreError, WriteValidationError, SchemaValidationError, OverloadedError, ClosingError, DatabaseLockedError, CorruptionError, IncompatibleSchemaVersionError, MigrationError, EmbedderIdentityMismatchError, EmbedderDimensionMismatchError, ExtractorError, InvalidFilterError, InvalidArgumentError).

### `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`
Four Rust unit tests on `validate_ffi_string`: `validate_ffi_string_accepts_plain_ascii` ("hello"), `validate_ffi_string_accepts_non_ascii_utf8` ("h├®llo ­¤”Ć µ¢ćÕŁŚ"), `validate_ffi_string_rejects_embedded_nul` ("a\0b" ŌåÆ err contains "NUL"), `validate_ffi_string_rejects_lone_surrogate` (uses `"\u{FFFD}"` since a real surrogate can't appear in a Rust &str; exhaustive surrogate guard sits in the Python layer).
